prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for nitrobenzene (C₆H₅NO₂) were computed using **DFT at tier standard (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fit) in PySCF**, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The Fukui indices (f⁺ for electrophilicity, f⁻ for nucleophilicity, and f⁰ for radical reactivity) were derived from Mulliken charges of the neutral, cation (+1), and anion (−1) states. All states converged with charge-conservation checks passed (Σf⁺ = 1.0000, Σf⁻ = 1.0000). The most electrophilic site is atom 4 (ortho-carbon, f⁺ = 0.496), and the most nucleophilic site is atom 5 (meta-carbon, f⁻ = 0.107). The oxygen atoms of the nitro group are also highly electrophilic (f⁺ ≈ 0.473 each), while hydrogen atoms are uniformly nucleophilic. The dual descriptor reveals the competitive electrophilic vs. nucleophilic character at each position, with the nitro group strongly polarizing the aromatic ring.</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true
